name: Poolside Central Studio
subtitle: Balcony & Beach Walk
tagline: Perfectly located — the beach is your backyard
welcomeMessage: Welcome to the Poolside Central Studio! This chic, compact studio is perfectly positioned right in the heart of Protaras, a short stroll from the beach and all the best restaurants and cafés. Everything you need is here — fresh linens, a fully equipped kitchen, a sunny balcony, and a shared pool to cool off in. Make yourself at home and enjoy every sun-soaked moment!

address.display: 3B Helios Court, Protaras Avenue, Protaras, Cyprus
address.nearestLandmark: 8 min walk to Fig Tree Bay

checkin.keyLocation: Reception desk in the lobby (staffed 8am–8pm) or key drop box for late arrivals
steps: Enter the main building through the glass doors on Protaras Avenue
The studio is on the ground floor — door number 3B on the left
Use the key provided at reception to open the studio door
For after-hours arrival, use the key drop box (code: 4321) next to the lobby door
The pool access card is in the welcome envelope on the kitchen counter
checkin.notes: The shared pool is open 8am–9pm. Pool towels are NOT provided — please bring your own beach towels. The pool area has sunbeds for all guests.

gettingHere.directions: From Larnaca Airport, take the A3 east and exit at Paralimni/Protaras. Follow Protaras Avenue into the centre of Protaras. Helios Court is on the right, just past the Protaras roundabout, next to the pharmacy. Look for the small sign at the entrance.
gettingHere.parking: Street parking available on Helios Court and surrounding roads — free and usually easy to find. No dedicated space.
gettingHere.publicTransport: Buses 101 and 102 stop directly on Protaras Avenue, 2 minutes walk. Frequent service from Ayia Napa and Larnaca.
gettingHere.taxi: Recommended: City Cab Protaras (+357 23 833 000). Taxis also available at the Protaras roundabout 5 min walk away.

houseRules: No parties or events — quiet hours 11pm to 8am
Clean dishes immediately and keep the kitchen counter clear — Cyprus's warm weather can attract insects
Please use the bin next to the toilet — do not flush toilet paper (Cyprus plumbing cannot handle it)
Place the metallic key in the electricity plug by the door when inside; remove it every time you leave
Shared pool: no glass, no diving, be mindful of other guests
Close balcony door and windows when air conditioning is running
Return keys to the keybox and reset the code at checkout
Treat the apartment with care — please report any breakages promptly

appliances.name: Air Conditioning
appliances.instructions: Wall-mounted AC unit above the door. Use the remote on the windowsill. Press ON/OFF, select COOL, and set to 22°C. The unit is powerful for the room size — 23–24°C may feel more comfortable. Always switch off when leaving and keep the balcony door closed.

appliances.name: Smart TV
appliances.instructions: Mounted on the wall. Press the power button on the remote or on the TV itself. Netflix is available — use our guest profile. Plug in your HDMI dongle (box under TV) to mirror your phone. For satellite TV, press SOURCE and select HDTV.

appliances.name: Induction Hob
appliances.instructions: Touch the ON button in the centre of the hob to activate. Tap the zone you want to use and press + to increase heat level. Use 3–4 for most cooking, 7–9 for boiling. Always use the induction-compatible pots and pans provided. Wipe the surface clean after each use with the cloth in the drawer.

appliances.name: Coffee Machine
appliances.instructions: Nespresso Essenza on the counter. Capsules are in the small wicker basket beside it — 6 complimentary capsules are provided. Fill the water tank at the back. Place a cup under the nozzle, insert a capsule, lock the lever, and press the lungo or espresso button.

items.name: Venus Beach
items.tip: Sandy beach perfect for swimming and sunbathing. Famous for some of the most beautiful sunsets on the whole island!

items.name: Faros (Lighthouse) Beach
items.tip: Calm atmosphere with sun beds and umbrellas. Crystal-clear waters — great for families and quiet days by the sea.

items.name: Alykes Beach
items.tip: Lively beach with easy access to cafes and restaurants along the waterfront.

items.name: White River Beach
items.tip: A hidden gem with stunning natural beauty. Unique zen stone art installations scattered around make this place truly special.

items.name: Tombs of the Kings
items.tip: UNESCO World Heritage site. Ancient tombs carved from solid rock — a unique look at Paphos's rich history.

items.name: Paphos Castle & Harbor (Limani)
items.tip: Historic castle right by the harbour — lovely for exploring and waterfront dining.

items.name: Harbor Promenade
items.tip: Follow the promenade from the Castle along the Limani for scenic views, shops and dining. Especially charming at sunset. 🌅

items.name: Blue Lagoon (Akamas)
items.tip: Breathtaking turquoise waters — ideal for snorkelling and swimming. Take a boat trip from Latchi harbour.

items.name: Paphos Wreck (MV Demetrios II)
items.tip: Shipwreck visible from shore near Chloraka. A favourite for photographers — adds a touch of maritime mystery to the coastline.

items.name: Aphrodite's Rock (Petra tou Romiou)
items.tip: The legendary birthplace of Aphrodite. Iconic rock formation with fantastic photo opportunities.

items.name: Akamas Peninsula
items.tip: Nature reserve ideal for hiking, with the Blue Lagoon accessible by foot trail or boat.

items.name: Troodos Mountains
items.tip: Stunning mountain scenery, great for hiking and exploring traditional villages. A world apart from the coast.

items.name: Wine Tasting
items.tip: Nearby wineries offer tastings and tours. Try Vouni Panayia Winery for an authentic local wine experience.

items.name: Avakas Gorge
items.tip: Scenic 2–3 hour hike through impressive rock formations and diverse flora. Best enjoyed early in the morning.

items.name: Kremiotis Waterfall
items.tip: Hidden waterfall near Kritou Terra — peaceful hike through lush surroundings. Especially beautiful after the rainy season.

items.name: Aphrodite & Adonis Nature Trails
items.tip: Interconnected trails with breathtaking coastal views and views of the Blue Lagoon below. Start near the Baths of Aphrodite. Moderately challenging.

eatAndDrink.name: O Firis
eatAndDrink.tip: Modern twist on Cypriot cuisine with generous portions at great prices. Live music almost every evening. Very popular — book ahead!

eatAndDrink.name: Georgia
eatAndDrink.tip: Very local and authentically Cypriot family-run restaurant. Simple, honest food at great prices.

eatAndDrink.name: Three Little Pigs
eatAndDrink.tip: Local favourite with high-quality food, great service, and a lovely atmosphere. Their house wine is a treat!

eatAndDrink.name: Psarou Fish Tavern
eatAndDrink.tip: Cozy tavern known for fresh fish, traditional meze, and a friendly local vibe.

eatAndDrink.name: Kingfisher Taverna
eatAndDrink.tip: Small, relaxed taverna serving high-quality fish dishes and local favourites in a more upscale setting.

eatAndDrink.name: West End Hotel & Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Family-run with generous portions and delicious seafood — steps from the sea.

eatAndDrink.name: Lidl
eatAndDrink.tip: Budget-friendly with a wide range of groceries. Great for stocking up on basics.

eatAndDrink.name: AlphaMega
eatAndDrink.tip: Large supermarket with a wide variety of fresh produce and local Cypriot items.

shopping.name: Kings Avenue Mall
shopping.tip: The main shopping mall in Paphos. Wide range of shops, restaurants and a cinema.

shopping.name: Paphos Market
shopping.tip: Covered marketplace with stalls for clothing, bags, jewelry, beach gear and local souvenirs.

shopping.name: Old Town
shopping.tip: Wander through the beautiful Old Town and discover many small boutique stores and artisan shops.

gettingAround.name: Bus
gettingAround.info: The central bus station (Tombs of the Kings) is a 10-min walk away, with direct connections to main attractions, the airport, and nearby towns.

gettingAround.name: RideNow (Car Sharing)
gettingAround.info: Car sharing works well around Paphos, including to and from the airport. Download the app, sign up in 5 minutes. Cars typically available within a 5–10 min walk.

gettingAround.name: Bolt (Taxi)
gettingAround.info: Budget-friendly taxi app. Recommended for getting around Paphos without the uncertainty of metered cabs.

contacts.name: Emergency Services
contacts.note: Police, Ambulance, Fire — free from any phone

contacts.name: Paphos District Hospital
contacts.note: Main hospital in Paphos — approx. 10 min drive

emergency.nearestHospital: Paphos District Hospital — approx. 10 min drive. Ask Google Maps for 'Paphos General Hospital'.
emergency.nearestPharmacy: Several pharmacies within walking distance on the main road. Ask us if you need a recommendation.

steps: Remove all items from the fridge and dispose of leftover food
Strip the bed and leave linens folded at the foot of the bed
Wash any dishes used and leave them drying on the rack
Empty the rubbish bin and take the bag to the wheelie bins in the car park
Close all windows and the balcony door
Switch off the air conditioning, all lights, and the TV
Return the room key and pool card to reception (or leave on the counter for late departure)
WhatsApp us to let us know you've left — thanks so much for staying!
checkout.lateCheckout: Late checkout is generally not available due to our tight housekeeping schedule. Please let us know if you have a special circumstance and we'll try our best.
